div.p: William, king of the Scots, to all the worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the half ploughgate of land which Richard of London has given to them in Lessudden, to be held in free and everlasting alms, as well and fully, quitely and honourably, as the charter of Richard himself to them bears witness. As witnesses, David my brother, Nicholas the chancellor, Alan son of the Steward, William de la Haye, Hugh de Essarts, William de Mortimer, Robert son of Jordan. At Selkirk.
